title: LAPD lets contract with surveillance giant Flock expire
titleZh: 洛杉矶警方不再续签与监控巨头Flock的合同
author: forks
relevanceReason: 命中 2 个 AI/科技关键词。
summaryRaw: The Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) is reportedly ending its deal with Flock Safety, a surveillance company that helps law enforcement track vehicles using thousands of its license plate cameras placed across the United States.

A senior LAPD official told news outlets, first reported by ABC7 and the Los Angeles Times, that the police department would allow its three-year contract with Flock to expire when it ends on Saturday. The department cited “serious concerns” around civil liberties and privacy. Flock’s cameras are operated by the Atlanta, Georgia-based company and not the LAPD.

“This contract is not being renewed because of serious concerns around civil liberties and civil rights issues, particularly around privacy and the data that is being collected from these cameras,” LAPD’s chief information officer Dean Gialamas was quoted as saying. “The LAPD had to make a difficult decision, in this case discontinuing using Flock services until we can get those data, privacy, security and sharing concerns ironed out through a contractual relationship.”

A spokesperson for the LAPD did not respond to a request for comment from TechCrunch over the weekend, and it’s unclear if Flock’s cameras will continue recording in absence of an active contract. According to ABC7, the police department is seeking new language in its contract addressing privacy and data storage concerns.

As the third-largest police department in the U.S., the LAPD is one of Flock’s largest government customers to date. Several major U.S. cities have also stopped working with Flock, including Mountain View, California and South Portland, Maine, citing privacy worries and concerns that federal immigration officials used the cameras to track people in violation of their local laws governing their sanctuary city policies.
summaryZh: The Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) is reportedly ending its deal with Flock Safety, a surveillance company that helps law enforcement track vehicles using thousands of its license plate cameras placed across the United States.

A senior LAPD official told news outlets, first reported by ABC7 and the Los Angeles Times, that the police department would allow its three-year contract with Flock to expire when it ends on Saturday. The department cited “serious concerns” around civil liberties and privacy. Flock’s cameras are operated by the Atlanta, Georgia-based company and not the LAPD.

comments.author: etdznots
comments.textRawHtml:
The best part is that flock owns the cameras and the poles so even when the contract expires the cameras keep running and recording data that flock can sell to e.g. CHP, LASD, FBI, Palantir; and LAPD can just call them and access the data

the flock scam was engineered to be resilient to political pressure by giving departments and jursidictions this fake exit ability while the data continues to be harvested, it is a noose that only tightens; the amount of flock cameras recording only ever goes up not down.
comments.textZhHtml:
最好的部分是，flock 拥有摄像机和杆，因此即使合同到期，摄像机仍会继续运行并记录数据，flock 可以将其出售给例如CHP、LASD、FBI、Palantir；洛杉矶警察局可以打电话给他们并访问数据

羊群骗局的设计目的是为了抵御政治压力，通过向各部门和司法管辖区提供虚假的退出能力，同时继续收集数据，它是一个只会收紧的绞索；集群摄像机的记录数量只增不减。

children.author: smalltorch
children.textRawHtml:
Yeah it's kinda crazy you can't legally take them down even if they are banned/contract expires. IKE Skelton, a county commissioner took it into his own hands and they were pressing felony charges on him. Not sure what ended up happening. Basically flock wouldn't respond to take them down, he felt it was his duty to remove them, he brought them back to his office, and then the state hunted him down.

Here is a podcast about it. https://internationalflavor.podbean.com/e/the-surveillance-s...
children.textZhHtml:
是的，这有点疯狂，即使它们被禁止/合同到期，你也无法合法地删除它们。艾克·斯克尔顿 (IKE Skelton) 是一名县专员，他亲自处理了此事，他们对他提出重罪指控。不确定最终发生了什么。基本上羊群不会回应将它们拿下，他觉得移除它们是他的责任，他将它们带回他的办公室，然后国家追捕他。

这是一个关于它的播客。 https://internationalflavor.podbean.com/e/the-surveillance-s...

children.author: glaslong
children.textRawHtml:
I'm curious how they could prevent taking them down if the local gov doesn't renew the contract? Presumably they're installed under some works dept land/pole/utility access permits that allow them the space and electrical, which all goes away and requires their removal.

Sorry if this is answered in the pod, don't have time for it immediately.
children.textZhHtml:
我很好奇，如果当地政府不续签合同，他们如何才能阻止拆除它们？据推测，它们安装在一些工程部门的土地/电线杆/公用设施访问许可证下，允许它们使用空间和电力，这些都消失了，需要将其拆除。

抱歉，如果在吊舱中回答了这个问题，我没有时间立即解决。

children.author: smalltorch
children.textRawHtml:
Id have to re-listen as well for all the details. But I think this is slightly different than the headline here.

In this case, the county voted for an ordinance banning them. Ike was threatened saying your going to be charged this is potentially state property, he did a sunshine request to see that they were privately owned by flock. Then he requested flock take them down but they didn't. After a few months he decided he will enforce the ordinance as the sheriff refused too.

He took them down brought them to his office. Then later 5 state officers (4 in plain clothing, one in uniform) were looking for him at his house. He brought them to the cameras and said here have them back.

Still got charged with theft somehow...

Moral of the story, that doesn't really sound like democracy to me. That sounds like kinda the opposite of democracy.

Anyway it's worth a listen if you have time. This isn't how these things should go and shows there is a little more than meets the eye here. Even if citizens perfectly execute democracy, these things may not budge. And there is a larger net of protection keeping these in place.
children.textZhHtml:
我还必须重新聆听所有详细信息。但我认为这与这里的标题略有不同。

在这种情况下，该县投票通过了一项禁止它们的法令。艾克受到威胁说你将被指控这可能是国家财产，他提出了一个阳光要求，以确保它们是羊群私人拥有的。然后他要求羊群把它们拿下来，但他们没有。几个月后，他决定执行该法令，但治安官也拒绝了。

他把它们拿下来带到他的办公室。随后，5名州官员（4名便衣，1名制服）到他家寻找他。他把它们带到摄像机前，说在这里把它们拿回来。

仍然被指控盗窃……

这个故事的寓意是，对我来说，这听起来并不像民主。这听起来有点与民主相反。

无论如何，如果你有时间，还是值得听一听。这些事情不应该这样发展，这表明事情远比表面上看到的要多一些。即使公民完美地执行民主，这些事情也可能不会改变。并且有更大的保护网将它们保持在适当的位置。

children.author: alhirzel
children.textRawHtml:
Didn't know about sunshine request - the website[1] - until I searched for this term!

[1]: https://www.sunshinerequest.com/
children.textZhHtml:
不知道阳光请求 - 网站[1] - 直到我搜索这个词！

[1]：https://www.sunshinerequest.com/

children.author: tptacek
children.textRawHtml: This is just FOIA. You don't need any special website or process; just Google [(your state) FOIA]  or [(your municipality) FOIA officer]. In Illinois, you can simply email free-form requests for documents and start a 10-day clock on the public body's side.
children.textZhHtml: 这只是FOIA申请。你不需要什么特别的网站或流程；直接搜索“[你所在的州] FOIA”或“[你所在的市] FOIA官员”就行。在伊利诺伊州，你只需发一封邮件，随意写明需要哪些文件，公共机构那边的10天处理倒计时就开始了。

children.author: alright2565
children.textRawHtml:
The problem is following up. It's hard to understand the process, and what to do when the public body doesn't respond.

If nothing else, the Sunshine Request site is a good place to get form emails for these requests from.
children.textZhHtml:
问题在于后续跟进。很难理解整个流程，也不知道当公共机构不予回应时该怎么办。

退一步讲，Sunshine Request网站是获取此类申请模板邮件的好去处。

children.author: chaps
children.textRawHtml:
What you do is: you sue. On my 13th FOIA lawsuit and I have a couple more in the works ;)

Depending on where you're from, it's pretty easy to find a pro bono attorney. In Illinois, the plaintiff's attorneys recoup their costs on a successful win.
children.textZhHtml:
你要做的就是：起诉。我正在打第13起FOIA诉讼，手里还有几起在筹备中；)

这取决于你所在的地区，找一位公益律师其实挺容易的。在伊利诺伊州，原告律师在胜诉后可以收回诉讼成本。

children.author: tptacek
children.textRawHtml: Whatever Chaps says about this, take to the bank.
children.textZhHtml: 关于此事，无论Chaps说什么，你都大可深信不疑。

children.author: smalltorch
children.textRawHtml:
Im not sure if there is a way to verify that the one he is talking about, but for public records request muckrock is great.

https://www.muckrock.com
children.textZhHtml:
我不确定有没有办法核实他说的是不是那个，不过对于公共记录申请来说，MuckRock非常好用。

https://www.muckrock.com

children.author: toomuchtodo
children.textRawHtml: MuckRock is the gold standard for systematizing FOIA requests imho.
children.textZhHtml: 在我看来，MuckRock是将FOIA申请系统化的黄金标准。

children.author: chasd00
children.textRawHtml:
> he did a sunshine request to see that they were privately owned by flock. Then he requested flock take them down but they didn't. After a few months he decided he will enforce the ordinance as the sheriff refused too.

yeah that's basically theft then. The cameras are probably a lot of money and so the dollar number put it in felony territory.
children.textZhHtml:
> 他做了一个阳光请求，希望看到它们是羊群私有的。然后他要求羊群把它们拿下来，但他们没有。几个月后，他决定执行该法令，但治安官也拒绝了。

是的，这基本上就是盗窃。这些相机可能价值不菲，因此其金额已构成重罪。

children.author: moron4hire
children.textRawHtml: Moral of the story: never talk to the police. Even if you yourself are police.
children.textZhHtml: 这个故事的寓意：永远不要与警察交谈。即使你自己是警察。

children.author: cucumber3732842
children.textRawHtml:
>Still got charged with theft somehow...

More equal animal acting in his official capacity gets treated like less equal animal.... basically.
children.textZhHtml:
>结果还是被控盗窃了……

更“平等”的动物在执行公务时，待遇却还不如那些“较不平等”的动物……大概就是这么个意思。

children.author: ryukoposting
children.textRawHtml:
> I'm curious how they could prevent taking them down if the local gov doesn't renew the contract?

IANAL but based on the facts available to me, they can't. It's a sham held up by intimidating local officials. The cameras were installed on public property, that's that.

If they somehow keep this nonsense running for very long, I'd anticipate a Meigs Field-esque incident at some point.
children.textZhHtml:
> 我很好奇，如果当地政府不续签合同，他们如何才能阻止拆除它们？

IANAL 但根据我掌握的事实，他们不能。这是恐吓地方官员所捏造的骗局。这些摄像头安装在公共财产上，就是这样。

如果他们以某种方式让这种胡言乱语持续很长时间，我预计在某个时候会发生梅格斯菲尔德式的事件。

children.author: chasd00
children.textRawHtml: If the cameras and poles are private property then, contract or not, taking them is theft is it not? You'll get in trouble taking lime scooters and throwing them in the lake regardless of whatever contract exists or doesn't exist.
children.textZhHtml: 如果摄像头和电线杆是私有财产，那么不管有没有合同，拿走它们不就是盗窃吗？不管有没有什么合同，你把Lime滑板车扔进湖里照样会惹上麻烦。

children.author: ryukoposting
children.textRawHtml:
The cameras and poles are private equipment installed on public property. If the city terminates the contract and says "get rid of it" and the contractor says "no," well someone has to deal with it.

Imagine if a power company got cleared to bury a bunch of power lines, but they left all the unused poles in the ground, on land they no longer have rights to. That's closer to the situation we're dealing with here.

Wireless and solar make some of the more visceral approaches to this problem ineffective. In the past, the city could have strongarmed Flock by severing power or data service somewhere on the public side.

I'd bet there are still tons of tricks LA can pull, though. These 1000 9-square foot patches of land have been rezoned as green space, we're clearing it for native plant life.

Or, like I said, just pull a Daley and remove them. The city owns the land. Flock can complain, they can sue, and they might even win. But once the city removes the cameras, Flock can't put them back. The city owns the land, and Flock has no rights to it.
children.textZhHtml:
摄像头和电线杆是安装在公共财产上的私有设备。如果市政府终止合同并要求“把它们拆了”，而承包商说“不”，那总得有人来处理这事。

想象一下，如果一家电力公司获批埋设一批电缆，但他们把所有废弃的电线杆都留在地里，而那块地他们已经没有使用权了。这跟我们这里的情况更接近。

无线和太阳能技术让一些更简单粗暴的解决手段失效了。过去，市政府可以通过切断公共区域的电源或数据服务来强行逼迫Flock公司就范。

不过我敢打赌，洛杉矶还是有一大堆招数可以用的。这1000块9平方英尺的土地已经被重新规划为绿地，我们正在清理它们以恢复原生植物。

或者，就像我说的，直接学学戴利，把它们拆了。土地是市政府的。Flock可以抗议，可以起诉，甚至可能胜诉。但只要市政府把摄像头拆了，Flock就再也装不回去了。地是市政府的，Flock对这块地没有任何权利。
